node.title: FANGMULA Offer Concerns: Should I Still Join

article.title: FANGMULA Offer Concerns: Should I Still Join
article.content:
I recently got an offer from one of the FANGMULA companies, but they are not ready to consider my complete notice period, and my current company is not ready to reduce it because I am working on a few projects which will go live in a few months. Last week, I got a call from one of their senior managers stating that if I cannot reduce the notice period, I should look for another team. I was shocked and literally cried, but at around midnight, I received an email from the senior manager saying to go ahead with the candidate proposed (after serving my standard notice period) for the time being. However, I am not sure what will happen next, as one of the senior managers told me to look for another team, and in the email, they also mentioned “for the time being.”

They took one month to complete the background check and everything, and now they are not ready to proceed with the notice period.This company is also known for its PIP (Performance Improvement Plan) culture, which makes me even more uncertain about joining. The CTC they are offering is just a 2% increase in base and some stocks. Is it normal to have such behavior in FANGMULA companies?

post.content: Looks like it is Microsoft because OP mentioned longer BGV time and no major change in base component. By any chance it is related to E + D org?
continue with the current company and try  applying for other companies... thousands of companies are available to choose right now
How long is your notice period OP? I have 1st hand exp with 2 of the companies you tagged, they were fine with 2 months notice.

what they do in BGV??
And how log it takes to complete ??
And is it possible that after doing bgv , they can revoke the offer also??
